rulings.Case Title: WING WO MA VS. JUN TAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn, Memo Of P&A, Declaration And Exhibits In Support Of Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 31, 2013, at 9:30 am, line 1, DEFENDANTS JUN TAN AND LINDA HO'S MOTION, Memo Of P&A, Declaration And Exhibits In Support Of Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal Of Action Granted. No opposition filed. Dismissal was the result of mistake or excusable neglect of Cross-Complainant's counsel. Mr. Wolff did not file a response to the OSC on April 16, 2013 as he believed that the response filed to the OSC on December 12, 2012 was sufficient. (Wolff Decl., 13.) The case has not settled in its entirety. Cross-Complainants' claims against Wing Wo Ma and Balanced Buildings remain outstanding. (Id. at 12.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MEAD VS. SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Atty Fees
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, May 31, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANTS SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION, WINSTON VAUGHN, LAURA GOETZ, RICHARD PANELL, CHANDLER PROPERTIES, INC. AND NORMAN HALL'S Motion For Atty Fees Granted, for good cause shown. No opposition filed. The court awards moving parties their attorneys fees and costs incurred in the appeal, pursuant to CCP 425.16(c)(1) and 1033.5 in the following amounts: Attorneys Fees: Lewis, Brisbois : $18,726.25; Stratman Patterson : $2500; Statutory costs: $1088.69; fees and costs for filing this motion: $3000, for a total of $25,314.94. The Court will also enter judgment in favor of Defendants Chandler Properties, Inc. and Normal Hall for a total amount of $36,434.74. Moving parties are to prepare a proposed order and a separate proposed judgment. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motiona Dnm Otion For Appointment Of Arbitrator; Ccp 1281.6
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, May 31, 2013, Line 7, PETITIONER SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN'S Motion For Appointment Of Arbitrator; Ccp 1281.6 Denied. The parties agreed to binding arbitration with the Bar Association of San Francisco ("BASF"). This agreement is unenforceable under the BASF rules and the Mandatory Fee Arbitration Act. See Benjamin, Weill, & Mazer v. Kors (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 40, 54. The Court cannot now appoint an arbitrator or compel binding arbitration in a forum which differs from the one selected by the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: EGNACIO E.R. BALINTON VS. BODYMASTERS SPORTS INDUSTRIES INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue Sanctions To Preclude Evidence, Argument And Issues Against Defts Due To Fialure To Produce Documents And Evidence In Violation Of Court Order, And Discovery Reqiuests & Requests For Award Of Attorneys Fes And Costs Ccp 2031.320
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 31, 2013, Line 1. Plaintiff EGNACIO BALINTON'S Motion for Issue Sanctions to Preclude Evidence, Argument, and Issues Against Defendants Due to Failure to Produce Documents and Evidence in Violation of Court Order, and Discovery Requests & Requests for Award of Attorneys Fees and Costs CCP 2031.320 Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENZO CALOIARO et al VS. SCOTT SIDWELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S To Respond And Produce Documents Pursuant To Pltf'S Req. For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 31, 2013, Line 4. Plaintiff VINCENZO CALOIARO'S Motion to Compel Defendants to Respond and Produce Documents Pursuant to Plaintiff's Request for Production and Inspection of Documents and Things, Set Two Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff CALOIARO's Motion to Compel Defendants' to Respond and Produce Documents Pursuant to Plaintiff's RFPD, Set Two, is DENIED. Defendants timely responded to Plaintiff's RFPD, Set Two on April 26, 2013; Defendants subsequently produced documents on or about May 7, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: WWC SERVICES OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, May 31, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend to state a cause of action against defendant if plaintiff can do so in good faith. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.
